name: Eggplant in Szechwaun Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 large green onion, chopped into half-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut eggplant into 1" x 3-inch julienne strips.
2. Place the cut pieces in ice water for 15 minutes to cool and prevent discoloration.
3. Drain the eggplant thoroughly and pat dry with paper towels.
4. Heat a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat, add oil, and swirl to coat the pan.
5. Add the eggplant strips to the hot oil and stir to coat evenly. Cover and cook for 2 minutes.
6. Uncover and continue cooking for another 2 minutes until the eggplant is lightly browned on all sides.
7. Remove the eggplant from the heat and add chopped jalapenos, garlic, and green onion. Stir to combine.
8. Cook the mixture for an additional 20-30 minutes or until the eggplant pieces are completely limp and tender.
9. In a separate bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, water, white pepper (1/2 teaspoon initially), sugar, and cornstarch until smooth.
10. Pour the sauce over the cooked eggplant and green onion mixture, stirring to coat evenly.
11. Simmer for 1-2 minutes or until the sauce thickens slightly.
12. Transfer the dish to a serving dish and serve immediately at room temperature or warm.
[I have made this recipe several times with varying results. If you use very hot jalapenos and increase the white pepper to one teaspoon, the dish will be extremely spicy. For milder heat, use one jalapeno and less white pepper. The recipe is best served at room temperature for optimal flavor.]
